skills.name: flox-environments
skills.description: "Create reproducible, cross-platform development environments with Flox — a declarative environment manager built on Nix. ALWAYS use this skill when the user needs to: set up a project with system-level dependencies (compilers, databases, native libraries like openssl, libvips, BLAS, LAPACK); configure reproducible toolchains for Python, Node.js, Rust, Go, C/C++, Java, Ruby, Elixir, PHP, or any language; manage environments that must work identically across macOS and Linux; pin exact package versions for a team; run local services (PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka) alongside development tools; onboard new developers with a single command; or solve 'works on my machine' problems. Especially valuable for AI-assisted and vibe coding — Flox lets agents install tools into a project-scoped environment without sudo, system pollution, or sandbox restrictions, and the resulting environment is committed to the repo so anyone can reproduce it instantly. Use this skill even if the user doesn't mention Flox — if they describe needing reproducible, declarative, cross-platform dev environments with system packages, this is the right tool. Also use when the user mentions .flox/, manifest.toml, flox activate, or FloxHub."

commands.description: Restate requirements, assess risks, and create step-by-step implementation plan. WAIT for user CONFIRM before touching any code.

commands.name: pm2
commands.description: Analyze a project and generate PM2 service commands for detected frontend, backend, or database services.

commands.name: projects
commands.description: List known projects and their instinct statistics

commands.name: promote
commands.description: Promote project-scoped instincts to global scope

commands.name: prp-commit
commands.description: "Quick commit with natural language file targeting — describe what to commit in plain English"

commands.name: prp-implement
commands.description: Execute an implementation plan with rigorous validation loops

commands.name: prp-plan
commands.description: Create comprehensive feature implementation plan with codebase analysis and pattern extraction

commands.name: prp-pr
commands.description: "Create a GitHub PR from current branch with unpushed commits — discovers templates, analyzes changes, pushes"

commands.name: prp-prd
commands.description: "Interactive PRD generator - problem-first, hypothesis-driven product spec with back-and-forth questioning"

commands.name: prune
commands.description: Delete pending instincts older than 30 days that were never promoted

commands.name: python-review
commands.description: Comprehensive Python code review for PEP 8 compliance, type hints, security, and Pythonic idioms. Invokes the python-reviewer agent.

commands.name: quality-gate
commands.description: Run the ECC quality pipeline for a file or project scope and report remediation steps.

commands.name: refactor-clean
commands.description: Safely identify and remove dead code with verification after each change.

commands.name: resume-session
commands.description: Load the most recent session file from ~/.claude/session-data/ and resume work with full context from where the last session ended.

commands.name: review-pr
commands.description: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents

commands.name: rust-build
commands.description: Fix Rust build errors, borrow checker issues, and dependency problems incrementally. Invokes the rust-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.

commands.name: rust-review
commands.description: Comprehensive Rust code review for ownership, lifetimes, error handling, unsafe usage, and idiomatic patterns. Invokes the rust-reviewer agent.

commands.name: rust-test
commands.description: Enforce TDD workflow for Rust. Write tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with cargo-llvm-cov.

commands.name: santa-loop
commands.description: Adversarial dual-review convergence loop — two independent model reviewers must both approve before code ships.

commands.name: save-session
commands.description: Save current session state to a dated file in ~/.claude/session-data/ so work can be resumed in a future session with full context.

commands.name: sessions
commands.description: Manage Claude Code session history, aliases, and session metadata.

commands.name: setup-pm
commands.description: Configure your preferred package manager (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun)

commands.name: skill-create
commands.description: Analyze local git history to extract coding patterns and generate SKILL.md files. Local version of the Skill Creator GitHub App.

commands.name: skill-health
commands.description: Show skill portfolio health dashboard with charts and analytics

commands.name: test-coverage
commands.description: Analyze coverage, identify gaps, and generate missing tests toward the target threshold.

commands.name: update-codemaps
commands.description: Scan project structure and generate token-lean architecture codemaps.

commands.name: update-docs
commands.description: Sync documentation from source-of-truth files such as scripts, schemas, routes, and exports.
